Dremio CDC

Visual User Guide

Getting Started 01 How It Works 02 Quick Start Configuration 03 Sources 04 Target 05 Mappings Operations 06 Status Dashboard 07 Alerts 08 Dead Letter Queue 09 Settings
VISUAL GUIDE

Dremio CDC

Stream database changes directly into Dremio — no Kafka, no Spark, no infrastructure overhead.

1

How It Works

Two write modes, any source, one lightweight daemon.

Mode A — Dremio SQL A

Connects to Dremio's REST API and issues MERGE INTO / DELETE statements. Simple setup, works with any Dremio edition. Best for moderate-throughput pipelines.

Mode B — Open Catalog B

Writes Iceberg data files directly via PyIceberg. High throughput, no SQL round-trips. Recommended for Dremio Cloud & Enterprise with Open Catalog.

SourceProtocolAuth
PostgreSQLLogical replication (WAL)Replication user + replication slot
MySQLBinlog streamingREPLICATION SLAVE privilege
MongoDBChange streamsStandard connection string
DynamoDBDynamoDB StreamsIAM role / static credentials
SQL ServerDebezium HTTPCDC-enabled DB + Debezium server
OracleDebezium HTTP (LogMiner)CDC-enabled DB + Debezium server
DB2Debezium HTTPCDC-enabled DB + Debezium server
SnowflakeChange tracking streamsStandard credentials
CockroachDBChangefeed (CDC)Replication user
2

Quick Start

Up and running in minutes with Docker.

1

Pull and run the Docker image

Dremio CDC ships as a single Docker container with the UI included.

docker run -d \
  -p 8085:8085 \
  -v ~/cdc-data:/app/data \
  --name dremio-cdc \
  mshainman/dremio-cdc:latest
2

Open the UI

Navigate to http://localhost:8085 in your browser.

3

Add a source, configure the target, click Start

See the sections below for a walkthrough of each page.

💡

The -v ~/cdc-data:/app/data volume mount persists your config, offsets, and DLQ across container restarts.

3

Sources

Connect to one or more databases. Each source can replicate multiple tables.

The Sources page lists all configured database connections. Each card shows the source type badge, connection name, and table count.

ℹ️

Click + Add source to open the connection wizard. The wizard walks you through connection details and table selection in two steps.

Step 1: Connection. Select source type, name the connection, and fill in host/port/database/credentials. PostgreSQL requires a replication slot name (default: dremio_cdc).

Oracle, SQL Server, and DB2 route through a Debezium server. The UI shows setup instructions inline — just copy the two commands shown, then enter the Debezium listen port.

  • ✦Multiple sources replicate simultaneously — one daemon handles all of them
  • ✦Each source independently tracks its replication offset
  • ✦Create in Dremio button auto-creates a source/namespace in Dremio from the sidebar
  • ✦Edit or delete any source without stopping other workers
4

Target

Tell the daemon where Dremio lives and how to write data.

Mode A — Dremio SQL

Mode A uses Dremio's REST API to issue MERGE INTO statements. Fill in host, port, user/password (or PAT for Dremio Cloud/Enterprise), and optionally the Project ID for Dremio Cloud. The Target Namespace is the Dremio source where CDC tables are created — it must be Iceberg-capable.

Mode B — Dremio Open Catalog

Mode B writes Iceberg files directly via PyIceberg. The Dremio connection fields become optional (used only for metadata refresh). No SQL round-trips — higher throughput, ideal for Dremio Cloud and Enterprise.

Optionally trigger a Transform Studio pipeline after each successful CDC flush. Enable the integration, point it at your Transform Studio URL, enter the pipeline ID, and optionally add a Bearer token if auth is enabled.

Configure the Iceberg REST catalog URI, warehouse/project name, Dremio PAT (for credential vending), and target namespace. For Dremio Open Catalog, credential vending is automatic — no S3 keys needed.

💡

For Dremio Cloud with Mode A: set the PAT field to your personal access token and fill in the Project ID (found in your Dremio Cloud project URL).

5

Mappings

See how source tables map to Dremio target paths. Spot and resolve collisions.

The Mappings page shows every source table → Dremio path mapping. A target collision warning appears when two sources write to the same Dremio path — common when replicating the same schema from multiple databases. Use the column picker (blue badge) to select which columns to replicate per table.

⚠️

Target collision means two sources are writing to the same Dremio table path. The last writer wins on each flush. Resolve by using different target namespaces per source or renaming the mappings in your config.

  • ✦Default path: namespace.schema_table (e.g. cdc.public_customers)
  • ✦Click a mapping row to override the target path or restrict to specific columns
  • ✦Column picker shows 4 cols ▼ badge when a subset is selected
  • ✦Click Refresh to re-derive mappings after adding new sources or tables
6

Status Dashboard

Real-time view of every replication worker — throughput, lag, batch sizing, and flush duration.

The Status page shows one card per source/table pair. Each card displays events processed, per-minute rate, current lag, batch size, flush duration, and time since last batch. The top bar shows aggregate totals and active worker count. Click Start/Stop to control the engine.

MetricWhat it means
EventsTotal INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE events processed since start
Per minCurrent throughput rate
LagSeconds behind the source's current position (WAL, binlog, stream)
BatchNumber of events grouped into the current flush batch
Flush msTime taken to write the last batch to Dremio
ℹ️

Workers show idle when waiting for new events (lag = 0). This is normal — it means the daemon is fully caught up. Engine: error in the top bar means a config issue prevented startup — check the config file path shown next to it.

  • ✦Prometheus metrics available at /metrics — link shown in the top-right of the Status page
  • ✦Reset offset button on each card rewinds that worker to replay from the beginning
  • ✦Auto-refresh every few seconds — no manual reload needed
7

Alerts

Get notified via Slack, webhook, or email when lag spikes or errors accumulate.

Configure lag and error thresholds, cooldown periods, and check interval. Add Slack webhooks, HTTP webhooks, or email recipients as notification channels. Recent alert history is shown at the bottom.

SettingDefaultDescription
Lag threshold30sFire if any worker's lag exceeds this value
Error count threshold3Fire after this many consecutive flush errors
Cooldown120sMinimum gap between repeat alerts for the same worker
Check interval15sHow often the alert engine polls worker status
  • ✦Disable alerts without losing config — the checkbox suppresses notifications but still computes thresholds
  • ✦Multiple channels can be added — Slack, webhook, and email can all fire on the same alert
  • ✦Recent alerts list shows the last few firings with worker name, metric, and timestamp
8

Dead Letter Queue (DLQ)

Failed batches are parked here — inspect, retry, or discard without losing data.

The DLQ shows failed batches with source, table, event count, status, retry count, error reason, and timestamp. Entries can be retried (re-submitted to the flush pipeline) or discarded. The summary cards at the top show counts by state: Pending, Exhausted, Replayed, and Discarded.

StatusMeaning
pendingFailed, waiting for manual retry or next auto-retry
exhaustedRetried 3 times — still failing, requires attention
replayedSuccessfully retried and written to Dremio
discardedManually dismissed — events not written
⚠️

Exhausted entries mean Dremio was unreachable or rejected the write 3 times. Fix the underlying issue (check Target settings, Dremio availability) then use Retry to re-submit.

9

Settings

Tune batching, snapshot behavior, offset storage, logging, and secrets management.

Batching & Snapshot

Batch size controls how many events are grouped per Dremio write. Adaptive batching auto-tunes the size based on throughput and lag (high load → larger batches, low load → smaller). Snapshot on first run does a full table scan before streaming begins — disable if your table is already in Dremio. Incremental snapshot reads in PK-ordered chunks so streaming can start sooner.

Offset Storage & Logging

Offset storage tracks replication positions. SQLite (default) is single-process only. Switch to PostgreSQL DSN format for multi-agent deployments where multiple CDC engines share offsets. Log level controls verbosity — INFO is recommended for production.

Secrets Management

Choose between storing credentials directly in config.yml or using environment variables (${VAR_NAME}) or HashiCorp Vault (vault:path#field) for any secret field. When Vault is selected, additional fields appear for Vault URL, token or AppRole auth, and a Test connection button.

SettingDescription
Batch sizeStarting events-per-flush; ignored when adaptive batching is on
Batch timeoutFlush even if batch size not reached (seconds)
Adaptive batchingAuto-tunes between Min and Max batch size based on lag
Snapshot on first runFull-table scan before streaming — disable if table already in Dremio
Incremental snapshotChunk-by-chunk scan using cursor column; streaming starts sooner
Offset DB path / DSNSQLite file path or postgresql://user:pw@host/db for multi-agent
Log levelDEBUG / INFO / WARNING / ERROR
